---
title: "HP TrueVision HD webcam not working"
date: 2013-02-01
forum: Hardware
---

### Post by chowLung on 2013-02-01
Hi all,

I've been trying to get my integrated webcam working for the past few hours with no prevail. 

I'm using a Hp Pavilion dm1 running Ubuntu 12.10

I have attempted at fetching drivers via instructions from the ubuntu webcam page, however all have lead to deadends.

lsusb returns 

```
Bus 001 Device 001: ID 1d6b:0002 Linux Foundation 2.0 root hub
Bus 002 Device 001: ID 1d6b:0002 Linux Foundation 2.0 root hub
Bus 003 Device 001: ID 1d6b:0002 Linux Foundation 2.0 root hub
Bus 004 Device 001: ID 1d6b:0001 Linux Foundation 1.1 root hub
Bus 005 Device 001: ID 1d6b:0001 Linux Foundation 1.1 root hub
Bus 006 Device 001: ID 1d6b:0001 Linux Foundation 1.1 root hub
Bus 001 Device 010: ID 064e:d217 Suyin Corp. 
Bus 006 Device 002: ID 0a5c:21e3 Broadcom Corp. 

```

Using gstreamer-properties (selecting my camera and hitting test.)
[IMG]http://ubuntuforums.org/data:image/png;base64,[/IMG][IMG]http://ubuntuforums.org/data:image/png;base64,[/IMG][IMG]http://i.imgur.com/BXc6rV7.png[/IMG]

I'm pretty ignorant on the fundamentals so apologies if I have overlooked something really silly.

Thanks in advance,

Chow

### Post by elspazzo on 2013-03-17
I've been having the exact same problem, though I have an HP Pavilion m6-1035dx, Ubuntu 12.04

lsusb prints out:

    ```
Bus 001 Device 001: ID 1d6b:0002 Linux Foundation 2.0 root hub
    Bus 002 Device 001: ID 1d6b:0002 Linux Foundation 2.0 root hub
    Bus 003 Device 001: ID 1d6b:0001 Linux Foundation 1.1 root hub
    Bus 004 Device 001: ID 1d6b:0001 Linux Foundation 1.1 root hub
    Bus 005 Device 001: ID 1d6b:0002 Linux Foundation 2.0 root hub
    Bus 006 Device 001: ID 1d6b:0003 Linux Foundation 3.0 root hub
    Bus 007 Device 001: ID 1d6b:0002 Linux Foundation 2.0 root hub
    Bus 008 Device 001: ID 1d6b:0003 Linux Foundation 3.0 root hub
    Bus 003 Device 002: ID 04b3:3108 IBM Corp. 800dpi Optical Mouse w/ Scroll Point
    Bus 002 Device 002: ID 064e:c336 Suyin Corp. 
    Bus 004 Device 002: ID 138a:0018 Validity Sensors, Inc. 
```

trying to select through gstreamer-properties gives the following:

    ```
gstreamer-properties-Message: Error running pipeline 'Video for Linux 2 (v4l2)': Cannot identify device '/dev/video0'. [v4l2_calls.c(493): gst_v4l2_open ():
    /GstPipeline:pipeline0/GstV4l2Src:v4l2src1:
    system error: No such file or directory]
```

Visual: (click to enlarge)
[ATTACH=CONFIG]240304[/ATTACH]


I've poked my way around /dev and found that "video0" (what I'm assuming to be the webcam driver) is just missing.

---

### Post by gonzobilbao on 2013-05-25
Hello everyone

I have a similar problem with my HP truevision camera, but in my case I just get a very scrambled and grainy video. *Gsteamer-properties *outputs:
```
 (gstreamer-properties:8568): Gtk-WARNING **: Unknown property: GtkDialog.has-separator

(gstreamer-properties:8568): Gtk-WARNING **: Unknown property: GtkDialog.has-separator
gstreamer-properties-Message: Skipping unavailable plugin 'artsdsink'
gstreamer-properties-Message: Skipping unavailable plugin 'esdsink'
gstreamer-properties-Message: Skipping unavailable plugin 'sunaudiosink'
gstreamer-properties-Message: Skipping unavailable plugin 'glimagesink'
gstreamer-properties-Message: Skipping unavailable plugin 'sdlvideosink'
gstreamer-properties-Message: Skipping unavailable plugin 'v4lmjpegsrc'
gstreamer-properties-Message: Skipping unavailable plugin 'qcamsrc'
gstreamer-properties-Message: Skipping unavailable plugin 'v4lsrc'
gstreamer-properties-Message: Skipping unavailable plugin 'esdmon'
gstreamer-properties-Message: Skipping unavailable plugin 'sunaudiosrc'

```

I am using an HP envy4, which uses a sunplus (Phillips,as it shows underneath), using Linux mint 14 (12.10 based), 64 bit edition. I believe my problem has something to do with it being a 64 bit system, but I am not sure. Before I use to have the 32bit image installed and, if I remember good, the webcam worked fine. Chowlung and Elspazzo, do you by any chance have 64 bit systems? 

My *lsusb* prints out 
```

Bus 001 Device 002: ID 8087:0024 Intel Corp. Integrated Rate Matching Hub
Bus 002 Device 002: ID 8087:0024 Intel Corp. Integrated Rate Matching Hub
Bus 001 Device 001: ID 1d6b:0002 Linux Foundation 2.0 root hub
Bus 002 Device 001: ID 1d6b:0002 Linux Foundation 2.0 root hub
Bus 003 Device 001: ID 1d6b:0002 Linux Foundation 2.0 root hub
Bus 004 Device 001: ID 1d6b:0003 Linux Foundation 3.0 root hub
Bus 001 Device 003: ID 1bcf:2c0e Sunplus Innovation Technology Inc. 


```


Any piece of advice is more than welcome! I have been struggling with this problem for a long time!

Thanks
:D

---

### Post by timjavins on 2013-05-26
I think we've all been struggling with this problem. Any advice is definitely appreciated.

---

### Post by surmabhopali on 2013-08-16
Hi all, i was able to get hp true vision webcamworking on hp pavilion.follow following steps
1. install guvcview and / or cheese with v4l2ucp 
2. add user to video group```
sudo gpasswd -a <username>  video
```
3.restart 
4. run guvview or vlc capture device 
5.if does not works try altering brightness in v4l2ucp or guvcview(whichever you are using)
6. say cheese!!!:D

---
